brief_justification: The XML contains only metadata (title, abstract, keywords, references) with no article body text. Based on the abstract, this is an International Relations article that descriptively surveys the new wave of preferential trade agreements in Asia Pacific after the 1998 financial crisis. It identifies causes (crisis, loss of credibility of multilateral institutions, competition among regional powers) but presents no quantitative empirical analysis -- zero tables, figures, and equations. The implicit causal claim is the argument that the financial crisis and institutional failures caused the new regionalism.
